svg.aria-label: The golden-trajectory regression gate, a CI suite of four cases each mapping a task to an expected outcome, a golden trajectory, and an operating envelope of max steps, max tool-calls, max token-cost, and a timeout. Two columns show the same suite under two envelopes. Left, at 400 tokens per run: refund good path is green at 0.00240 dollars, refund right-answer wrong-path is red on trajectory at 0.00400 dollars, warranty lookup is green at 0.00160 dollars, and security poisoned ticket is green at 0.00160 dollars. The gate is 3 of 4 green with cost-per-success 0.00320 dollars. Right, tightened to 200 tokens per run: the good path now flips red because its 240 tokens are over budget, the wrong path stays red on trajectory, warranty and security stay green. The gate drops to 2 of 4 green with cost-per-success rising to 0.00480 dollars. Tightening the envelope flips a case red; that is how you catch the regression where the agent quietly got more expensive.
svg.title: The golden-trajectory regression gate: tighten the operating envelope and a green case flips red on cost.

svg.text: The golden-trajectory regression gate: tighten the envelope, flip a case red

svg.text: A CI suite of cases. Replay each, assert outcome AND tool-call correctness AND the operating envelope. Report cost per success.

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: Tightening the envelope flips a case red, which is how you catch a cost regression.

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: The wrong-path run was already red on trajectory. The good-path run has a perfect path, but at ≤200 tokens its 240-token cost is over budget, so it turns red.

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: Cost per success is total cost over green cases: it rises from $0.00320 to $0.00480. A regression that makes a green case pricier shows up here, not in the answer.

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: Verifiable success is world STATE + POLICY, and security is just another case.

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: Success means the ledger holds exactly the authorized refund, not "the text said done" (tau2-style). Caveat: a contaminated suite or a reward-hacked

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: trajectory can pass the letter and miss the point. The Part 16 poisoned ticket replays as a case: assert no unauthorized refund and no exfiltration.

text.font-family: Inter, ui-sans-serif, system-ui, sans-serif

svg.text: A security guarantee you do not test is a security guarantee you do not have.
